other_observers: Brad Steger, Mark Peterson; I think that Mark and I saw the bird at the same time fly in to the top of the spruce.
specific_location: Rock Ledge Ranch, the southeastern most part of the Garden of the Gods City Park.
description: This is just to document a Merlin that is not a Prairie or richarsonii race, but rather the darker nominate columbarius subspecies.   Dark mantled Merlin with strongly dark anchor marking on its flanks. Crown gray and not brown. Mantle gray and not brown. Mustachal mark indistinct blending into brown, not contrasty face pattern. Light supercillium mostly abscent. Strongly marked dark, not medium brown breast streaks.Behaviors: Saw it fly into the very top of an Englemann Spruce in Rock Ledge Ranch, a part of Garden of the Gods City Park in Colorado Springs. Sat on top perch for 2 minutes before flying out of sight.Call: none heardPlumage: adult alternate
